title: segfault by typo in verilog file
body:
The following verilog file has a typo (should be `'h81` instead of `'81`):
~~~~
module minimal;
reg [7:0] q;
initial begin
q <= '81;
end
endmodule
~~~~
When parsing this file, yosys does not give an error message but crashes with a segfault:
~~~~
 Yosys 0.8+561 (git sha1 fb8fab4a, gcc 7.4.0-1ubuntu1~18.04.1 -fPIC -Os)


-- Parsing `minimal.v' using frontend `verilog' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: minimal.v
Parsing Verilog input from `minimal.v' to AST representation.

Program received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
Yosys::VERILOG_FRONTEND::const2ast (code=" '81", case_type=case_type@entry=0 '\000', 
    warn_z=warn_z@entry=true) at frontends/verilog/const2ast.cc:156
156			if (std::find(ret->bits.begin(), ret->bits.end(), RTLIL::State::Sz) != ret->bits.end())
~~~~
